name: Lookout Mountain Lariat
_osm_road_widths_doc: Real public-road loop — per-vertex curb-to-curb widths map-matched from OSM lanes, same as Sand Creek. Matters here because the lap spans three very different roads: the ~6.5 m Lariat Trail switchbacks, 2-lane US-40 with shoulders, and one carriageway of 4-lane US-6.

source._doc: No KML — the whole loop lives on the OSM road network, fetched by route.roads. 'ref:' entries match numbered highways whose ways are unnamed in OSM (rural US routes); motorways are excluded so US-40/US-6 refs never drag I-70 in.

route._start_at_doc: Station 0 = the Lariat Trail stone gates on 19th St (the route doc's start/finish gantry). Also keeps the pit boxes off the US-6 interchange gore, where AC_PIT_2 previously spawned 1.3 m off-road.
route._width_profile_doc: Rural width table in widths_from_osm (3.0 m lanes, no urban parking/turn allowances): Lariat Trail ~6.5 m, US-40 ~9 m, one US-6 carriageway ~9 m — instead of the urban table's 11/18 m.
route._roads_doc: Paradise Road is the historic Lariat Loop leg through the Mount Vernon townsite — it tees off Lookout Mountain Rd and drops to US-40 at the exit-256 junction. Without it there is a 1.1 km hole (Lookout Mtn Rd itself never touches US-40). The id: entry is the US-6 northbound off-ramp at the 19th St interchange — 19th St crosses US-6 on a bridge, so closing the ring mainline-to-bridge directly creates a fake 6.5 m vertical step (a 17% launch ramp at start/finish); the lap must climb the real ramp.

wide_corners._doc: Kevin: 'the turn on the mountain route is impossible' — base-of-climb 90deg kink, 2-4 turns ahead of the start; same geometry as a switchback so it needs a human call

road_text._doc: No painted street-name decals — they land in the wrong places on this rural loop (Kevin), and mountain roads don't have them anyway.

road_profile._doc: Elevation is 100% real USGS 3DEP along the route — ~1,300 ft from the 19th St gates to the Buffalo Bill summit (~7,375 ft). No hand-authored dips/cambers; the mountain provides them. max_grade_pct 16 clears the real steepest pitch (Paradise Rd -14.5%) — the default 6% cap flattened the descent and floated the road metres above the canyon floor.
road_profile._crossfall_doc: Camber is MEASURED (data/crossfall.json): lateral 1 m-DEM pairs -> cross-slope, median+mean smoothed, 1.5% deadband (flat where mild - not everywhere), capped 6% (~3.4 deg, no launching). 61.7% of the lap banked, mountain sections tilt into the hillside.

scenery._doc: Foothills pine forest: TurboSquid-derived pine sprite atlas (rendered imagery only, mesh never ships), packed HARD near the road (step 7 m, 4/station/side, bias 2.4 -> most trees within ~25 m of the verge), grass ground + GrassFX. Streetlights ON per Kevin: yellow, 96 m, dim wide pools.

fences._doc: summit plateau ranch land - split-rail both sides at the right-of-way line (Kevin: off the road a bit like irl)

pylons._doc: distribution line paralleling the plains legs, well off the road; module carries its own 134 m cable span (tower-only module; cables strung in the Blender pass)

road_signs._doc: Euro faces per Kevin: sharp-turn arrows at warning-barrier entries; 70 disc on the climb, 110 discs on the plains legs.

forest3d._doc: real 3D pines in the driver's corridor; billboards carry the slopes

layouts._doc: One layout: gates -> Lariat Trail switchback climb -> Buffalo Bill summit -> Lookout Mountain Rd south to US-40 (Mount Vernon, exit 256) -> US-40 down Mt Vernon Canyon past Heritage Square -> US-6 north -> 19th St -> gates. ~20.9 km, the Nordschleife-length ring.
same world, opposite direction — own grid/timing via dummies_reverse.json + spawn kn5

streetlight._doc: Wide DIM wash (Kevin: ~3x spread, no ground saturation). CSP RANGE scales deposited energy - widening range demands cutting intensity: 60 m range at 1.1, soft 155-deg cone, high gradient offset, damped specular.

kerb._doc: Real mountain road: NO kerbs. The curvature test classed the whole mountain as corners and lined 25 km of a 6.5 m lane with 5 cm vertical-lip rumble strips on both edges - Kevin's 'undriveable slop'. Flush paved shoulder only.

runoff._doc: No flat tarmac runoff aprons on a pitched mountainside - run-off here is dirt, trees, and guardrail (warning barriers stay).

1GRASS._doc: Alpine grass instead of dry dirt (Kevin) - mesh stays 1GRASS so physics, audits and GrassFX are untouched; GrassFX (lighting.grassfx) grows 3D blades on it.

LIGHTPOST._doc: the purpose-modeled lamp's own skin (emissive baked, opacity alpha)

road_markings._doc: Lake Murray look: solid double-yellow centre + white edge lines (ribbon.lane_markings port); road asphalt tiles at 4 m to match.

props._doc: Kevin's model pack: 4 m concrete barrier modules instanced along warning-barrier runs as collidable 1WALL geometry (replaces the procedural swept jersey).
